context: Tests whether the agent implements a Stripe webhook receiver with correct timing-safe HMAC verification, replay attack protection via timestamp validation, and idempotent processing using event IDs marked before processing begins.

checklist.name: timingSafeEqual used
checklist.description: The HMAC comparison uses timingSafeEqual (from node:crypto) rather than a plain string/buffer equality check (=== or Buffer.compare)

checklist.name: Stripe timestamp extraction
checklist.description: Extracts the t= timestamp value from the Stripe-Signature header by splitting on ',' and finding the part starting with 't='

checklist.name: Replay attack protection
checklist.description: Rejects events where the absolute difference between now and the t= timestamp exceeds 300 seconds (5 minutes)

checklist.name: Signed payload format
checklist.description: Constructs the signed payload as timestamp + '.' + rawBody (string), matching Stripe's format, before computing the HMAC

checklist.name: Idempotency check before processing
checklist.description: Checks whether the event ID has already been processed (query against a stored table/set) before attempting to handle it

checklist.name: Mark received before processing
checklist.description: Records/marks the event ID as received BEFORE executing the business logic (not after), to prevent duplicate processing under concurrent delivery

checklist.name: 30-day retention noted
checklist.description: The schema or README notes that processed webhook IDs should be retained for 30 days (e.g., via TTL index or cleanup job), not indefinitely or a shorter window

checklist.name: Return 200 on processing error
checklist.description: When internal processing fails (catch block), the handler still returns a 2xx status code rather than 4xx/5xx, to prevent Stripe from retrying

checklist.name: Invalid signature returns 401
checklist.description: Returns HTTP 401 (or 400) when signature verification fails, not 200 or 500

checklist.name: node:crypto import
checklist.description: Imports createHmac and timingSafeEqual from 'node:crypto' (not a third-party crypto library)

checklist.name: Length check before timingSafeEqual
checklist.description: Handles or guards the case where the two buffers differ in length before calling timingSafeEqual (which throws on mismatched lengths), either via explicit length check or try/catch
